artifact.description: Investigative article — 'Unfinished Business: 12 Years of Violations at Meridian Chemical and the Neighborhood Left Waiting'

ai_assistance.description: Analyzed 4,300 public regulatory filings to categorize violations, identify enforcement gaps, and generate timeline patterns
Cross-referenced facility violation timeline with county health department respiratory complaint data by ZIP code

review.notes: Editor reviewed all findings; made framing decision on health-data correlation; chose contextualized presentation over omission or unqualified inclusion
Reviewed for defamation risk; requested softened characterization of DEQ enforcement and inclusion of full Meridian statement
Managing editor approved publication after clarifying sourcing behind violation count

decisions.description: Decided to include the AI-identified correlation between violation spikes and respiratory complaint rates, presented with full methodological context, expert caveats, and a call for formal study — rather than omitting it or presenting it as a causal finding
decisions.context: AI identified a statistically notable correlation between facility violation spikes (2019, 2022) and elevated respiratory complaints in the adjacent ZIP code. However, the data does not control for confounders (highway pollution, seasonal variation, COVID-19), the ZIP code is too coarse for facility-specific attribution, and two independent researchers called the pattern 'suggestive but not conclusive.'

aiInputs.contribution: Identified temporal correlation between violation clusters and respiratory complaint spikes across two ZIP codes

decisions.rationale: The community has a right to know about a pattern that independent experts consider worth investigating. Omission would withhold potentially actionable information from a vulnerable population. Unqualified inclusion would imply causation not supported by the data. Contextualized presentation — framing the correlation as a question warranting formal study, with full disclosure of limitations — serves the public interest while maintaining journalistic standards.

decisions.description: Established and executed a stratified sample verification of AI document analysis — checking 60 of 387 identified violations against original source documents, including all 43 flagged enforcement gaps
decisions.context: The AI categorized 387 violations across 4,300 documents. The reporter needed to establish a known error rate before relying on the analysis for publication.

aiInputs.contribution: Produced the initial categorized violation dataset that was then sampled and verified

decisions.rationale: A 15% stratified sample (biased toward high-stakes findings) provides a defensible error estimate. Checking all enforcement-gap flags ensures the most consequential AI findings are fully verified. Discovered 7% misclassification rate and 1 false-positive enforcement gap (2% of flagged items), both corrected before publication.

decisions.description: Accepted legal counsel's two requested changes: softened DEQ characterization and included full Meridian spokesperson statement
decisions.context: Legal review identified potential defamation exposure in characterizing DEQ response as 'neglect' and in excerpting rather than fully quoting Meridian's statement
decisions.rationale: Both changes reduce legal risk without materially altering the story's findings. 'Gaps in follow-through' is factually accurate and less legally provocative than 'neglect.' The full Meridian statement gives the company fair representation.

entities.label: Published investigative article — Meridian Chemical investigation
County health department respiratory complaint data by ZIP code
AI-generated categorized violation dataset (387 violations, corrected after verification)
Reporter's interview notes — 14 residents, 4 employees, 2 inspectors, company spokesperson, county health officer

activities.description: AI analyzed 4,300 regulatory filings — categorized violations by type, date, severity, and enforcement follow-up

interaction.responseNotes: Reporter verified stratified sample of 60 violations against source documents. Found 7% misclassification rate and 1 false-positive enforcement gap. Corrected dataset from 43 to 42 unenforced violations and reclassified 3 violation types.

activities.description: AI cross-referenced violation timeline with ZIP-code-level respiratory complaint data

interaction.responseNotes: Correlation finding evaluated by reporter, data journalist, and two independent academic researchers. Consensus: pattern is suggestive but not conclusive. Reporter contextualized with confounders (highway, seasonal variation, COVID-19) and expert assessments. Published as a question warranting further study, not a causal claim.

activities.description: Reporter manually verified 60 AI-categorized violations against original DEQ documents
Reporter conducted on-the-ground interviews — residents, employees, regulators, company spokesperson, county health officer
Reporter wrote the article — all published text is human-authored
Editor reviewed draft, discussed correlation framing, made editorial decisions on presentation
Editor and reporter decided to include health-data correlation with full context and caveats
Legal counsel reviewed for defamation risk and requested two changes
Managing editor reviewed final draft, clarified sourcing, and approved publication

relationships.notes: Reporter defined analysis queries and evaluated outputs
Reporter defined cross-reference parameters and evaluated correlation findings with independent experts
Verified AI categorizations against original documents
Presented with full context and expert caveats
Verified and corrected AI analysis used as factual basis
Contextualized with expert caveats; presented as question, not conclusion
Primary source reporting — residents, employees, regulators, company
AI findings guided interview questions and areas of focus

policyEvaluations.notes: AI used for document analysis and pattern identification only — not for writing published text. All AI findings verified against primary sources. AI disclosure note included in published article. Compliant with newsroom policy sections 3.1 (permitted uses), 3.4 (verification requirements), and 4.2 (reader disclosure).

All factual claims supported by at least two independent sources. Statistical claims reviewed by data journalist. Health correlation presented with appropriate caveats and expert context. Company given full opportunity to respond. Methodology sidebar included.
Reviewed for defamation, privacy, and fair comment. Two revisions requested and accepted: softened DEQ characterization, included full Meridian statement. Cleared for publication.
